tool_args.label: New York City Department of Transportation

payload.entity_name: New York City Department of Transportation

payload.description: Proposed SBS corridor along Webster Avenue from The Hub to Williamsbridge, replacing Bx41 LTD with Bx41 SBS, with offset bus lanes, bus bulb stations, off-board fare collection, transit signal priority, and pedestrian safety improvements. Planned implementation 2013/14.

payload.to: Williamsbridge (White Plains Rd / E Gun Hill Rd)
payload.description: Existing Bx41 route corridor, 5.3 miles from The Hub to Williamsbridge. Within a 10-minute walk: 200,000 residents, 71% of households do not own a car, 61% of residents commute by transit. Carries over 22,000 daily riders.
Proposed SBS service on Webster Avenue replacing Bx41 LTD, with 1/2 mile stop spacing, off-board fare collection, running frequently all day from The Hub to Williamsbridge
Existing Bx41 Local service on Webster Avenue, no change to stop spacing, service every ~10 minutes
Local service on Third Avenue, replaces the Bx55 route (weekday only) with an all times route, local stops from The Hub to Fordham Plaza
Proposed limited-stop service on Third Avenue, replacing the Bx55 LTD. Local stops: Harlem 125th St to The Hub, limited stops: The Hub to Fordham Plaza.
Limited service on Third Avenue and Webster Avenue north of Fordham Plaza. Replaced by Bx15 LTD in proposed plan.
Local service on Bainbridge/Valentine Avenues, proposed to be rerouted with southern portion moved to Webster Avenue
Proposed SBS service from The Bronx to LaGuardia Airport. Off-board fare collection, same stops as Bx41 SBS north of The Hub, connects to 6 train.

tool_args.label: Offset bus lanes with bus bulb stations

payload.description: 4 miles of offset bus lanes with bus bulb stations along Webster Avenue. Replace one general travel lane with a bus lane in each direction.
payload.locations: Webster Avenue corridor from The Hub to Williamsbridge

locations_normalized.raw_text: Webster Avenue corridor from The Hub to Williamsbridge

payload.description: Off-board fare collection for the entire Bx41 SBS route, like the Bx12 SBS on Fordham Road
Transit signal priority for the entire Bx41 SBS route
Pedestrian safety improvements including curb extensions/neckdowns, pedestrian refuge islands/medians, and pedestrian neckdowns for the entire route
Banning left-turns at CBE on-ramp, rerouting NB vehicles to Ittner Pl, dual right-turning lanes for high vehicle volumes accessing the CBE, SB Bus-Only Queue Jump, convert Ittner Place to one-way
payload.location_text: Cross Bronx Expressway / Webster Avenue / Ittner Place intersection

normalized_location.raw_text: Cross Bronx Expressway / Webster Avenue / Ittner Place intersection

payload.description: Lengthened left-turn bay, simplified Valentine Avenue design, new pedestrian crossings, convert E 178 St to WB one-way for rerouted SB left-turns, banned SB left-turns at Webster Avenue
payload.location_text: E Tremont Avenue / Webster Avenue intersection

normalized_location.raw_text: E Tremont Avenue / Webster Avenue intersection

payload.description: Lengthened left-turn bays for heavy left-turning volumes, NB and SB right-turn bays to keep bus lane clear, banned EB and WB left-turns, saved signal time reallocated to Webster Avenue

tool_args.label: E Gun Hill Road intersection improvements

payload.description: Thru and right-turn only lanes improve traffic organization on congested approach, increased signal time for congested turning movements, curbside bus lane and Bus-Only RT signal phase helps buses get through intersection faster

normalized_location.raw_text: E Gun Hill Road / Webster Avenue intersection

Community Advisory Committee Meeting #1 for Webster Avenue SBS. Project introduction, identification of issues and opportunities along the corridor.
Community Advisory Committee Meeting #2 for Webster Avenue SBS. Corridor data analysis, three corridor design ideas.
Community Advisory Committee Meeting #3 for Webster Avenue SBS. Introduction to corridor offset bus lane design, design ideas for specific areas along the corridor.
Community Advisory Committee Meeting #4 for Webster Avenue SBS. This presentation itself. Agenda: Project background, corridor design, traffic analysis, bus service planning, next steps.
Public Open House #2 for Webster Avenue SBS. Part of Winter 2012/13 next steps.
